title: tarenna sambucina var. sambucina
intro: Tarenna sambucina var. sambucina is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the Indian snake root or Sambac wood. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating digestive problems. Tarenna sambucina var. sambucina is typically prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or extract, and is often used to treat various ailments, including coughs, colds, and skin infections.
